completed.title: All-adversity config (ncrc_adversity_all.yaml)
completed.detail: Created with all 7 vehicle adversity classes at probability=1.0 using state_8am.xml.gz rush-hour base. VRU excluded — no pedestrian agents in environment.

completed.title: Adversity fire logging — class-level tracking
completed.detail: Patched nade.py and adversity_manager.py to log adversity_class (e.g. LanechangeAdversity) on every fire event. Previously only logged predicted_collision_type which is shared across multiple classes.

completed.title: Fixed DefenceLogger crash in LanechangeAbortAdversity
completed.detail: Added missing store_valid_distance_offset() and get_last_valid_distance_offset() methods to defence_logger.py. Was crashing with AttributeError on every run.

completed.title: Construction zone netconvert bypass
completed.detail: Rewrote generate_enhanced_network() in connection_manager.py. Replaced 3-step netconvert pipeline with shutil.copy2(). Root cause: SUMO C++ validator rejects manually-inserted via-lanes in TLS junctions. Validated: exit code 0, TLS count 197=197, MD5 identical.

completed.title: 520toRapp Autoware map synced to cloud
completed.detail: Added pointcloud_map.pcd, 520toRapp_lanelet2.osm, and map_projector_info.yaml to autoware upload_maps.sh and sync_maps.sh. Pushed to B2 and committed to hht-dev branch.

completed.title: Launched observe-haotian dashboard
completed.detail: Built and deployed this dashboard to GitHub Pages (910834430.github.io/observe-haotian) with password protection, live clock, charts, and daily task tracking.

in_progress.title: Adversity coverage loop — Run #2 stuck
in_progress.detail: Vehicle deadlock at edge 4414481701#0 (speed 0.26 m/s, reroute blocked). Process consumed 73 GB RAM before being killed. Fix: reduce --time-to-teleport from 200 → 60.

todo.title: Fix vehicle deadlock and restart adversity loop
todo.detail: Reduce --time-to-teleport from 200 to 60 in ncrc_adversity_all.yaml so stuck vehicles teleport out faster. Restart test_all_adversities.py loop.

todo.title: Complete adversity coverage test
todo.detail: Run loop to completion. Confirm which of 7 adversity classes fire with rush-hour traffic. Expected hard ones: HeadonAdversity, TrafficRuleAdversity, MergeAdversity.

todo.title: End-to-end static adversity test
todo.detail: Run ncrc_static_adversity.yaml with new construction zone fix. Confirm stalled vehicle + construction zone deploy with no TLS errors.

todo.title: Filter route file to NCRC-only
todo.detail: Filter aa_with_types.rou.xml to ~15,621 routes (3.2%) overlapping NCRC edges → produce aa_ncrc_only.rou.xml for denser local AV traffic.

completed.title: DynamicTrafficManager — runtime BV spawning on NCRC
completed.detail: Built DynamicTrafficManager class (utils/dynamic_traffic_manager.py). Replaces static state_8am.xml.gz with runtime spawn/despawn of BVs around the AV on the 15 km NCRC circular route. Maintains 9 target BVs using suffix-route pattern. Integrated into NADEWithAV.on_step() and run_experiment_cnde.py via dynamic_traffic_cfg param. New config: ncrc_dynamic_adversity_all.yaml with aa_dynamic.sumocfg (no state file, begins at t=0).

completed.title: BV-BV collision mitigations (3 fixes)
completed.detail: (1) Same-batch spawn guard: added pending_positions dict + MAX_SPAWNS_PER_STEP=2 cap so vehicles spawned in the same 0.5s tick check each other for 25m clearance. (2) AdversityManager BV-BV exclusion: _has_nearby_bv_in_lane() skips adversity if another BV is within 15m in same lane, preventing cascade crashes from adversarial maneuvers. (3) pos_error watchdog in NDEController: aborts trajectory and restores IDM after 4 consecutive drift steps >1.5m, preventing libsumo segfault from invalid vehicle states.

completed.title: continue_on_av_collision enabled + headless validation
completed.detail: Added continue_on_av_collision: true to ncrc_dynamic_adversity_all.yaml. Ran 3 headless tests. Best run: full 600s completed, 12 AV-BV collisions, avg 9.6 BVs nearby, 2431 adversity fires (1262 TFL, 793 lcabort, 158 leader, 93 lanechange, 73 follower). BV-BV collision rate reduced 5× vs. original.

completed.title: Static adversity expansion — enhanced NCRC network
completed.detail: Created aa_fixed_enhanced.net.xml with emergency right-turn connections at junctions 62477148 and 62532012, enabling traffic rerouting when lane 0 is coned off. Two new scenario configs: ncrc_static_adversity.yaml (stalled car on 8974919181#0 + construction zone on 4411907471#0, t=0 no state file) and ncrc_construction_stalled_8am.yaml (same adversities on rush-hour state_8am base). ConnectionManager handles dynamic lane permission modification.

completed.title: Per-adversity isolated test configs (4 new YAMLs)
completed.detail: Created ncrc_test_headon.yaml, ncrc_test_tfl.yaml, ncrc_test_lcabort.yaml, ncrc_test_merge.yaml. Each enables exactly one adversity class at probability=1.0 on the full NCRC circular route with 8am state file. MergeAdversity fixed to location='highway' (ramp edges classified as highway). HeadonAdversity isolated for later validation (requires opposing-direction traffic). ncrc_adversity_diverse.yaml: GUI demo with 5 fireable adversities at probability=0.15 for visual observation.

completed.title: Adversity coverage summary — 5 of 7 confirmed fireable on NCRC
completed.detail: From headless 600s dynamic run: LanechangeAdversity ✅, LanechangeAbortAdversity ✅, LeaderAdversity ✅, FollowerAdversity ✅, TrafficRuleAdversity ✅. HeadonAdversity: requires opposing-traffic edge — unclear on NCRC circular route. MergeAdversity: requires ramp context — location fix applied, not yet tested.

in_progress.title: Static adversity configs — created but not yet run
in_progress.detail: ncrc_static_adversity.yaml and ncrc_construction_stalled_8am.yaml are ready. Need to run and confirm: (a) cones deploy correctly on enhanced network, (b) AV navigates past construction zone using emergency connection, (c) stalled vehicle blocks lane 0 and AV responds.

in_progress.title: Per-adversity tests — not yet executed
in_progress.detail: ncrc_test_headon, ncrc_test_tfl, ncrc_test_lcabort, ncrc_test_merge all created but not run. Need to confirm each fires against AV in at least one 300s run.

todo.title: Run static adversity scenarios
todo.detail: python scripts/run_experiment_cnde.py --config configs/simulation/ncrc_static_adversity.yaml. Verify cones deploy, AV bypasses via emergency connection, no TLS errors.

todo.title: Validate per-adversity test configs
todo.detail: Run each of ncrc_test_headon/tfl/lcabort/merge. Confirm fire events in adversity_diag.jsonl. HeadonAdversity may need a route that passes opposing-direction traffic.

todo.title: Filter route file to NCRC-only edges
todo.detail: Filter aa_with_types.rou.xml to routes overlapping NCRC edges → produce aa_ncrc_only.rou.xml for denser relevant background traffic near AV. Currently static BVs include city-wide routes.

todo.title: Investigate residual BV-BV collisions from static background traffic
todo.detail: 11 of 18 BV-BV collisions in 600s run are between static BVs (v_2021-09-* IDs from aa_with_types.rou.xml). These fire adversarial commands on vehicles near each other. Consider raising _MIN_BV_GAP or limiting adversity eligibility to BVs within a specific distance of AV.

completed.title: All 7 adversity classes AV-targeted
completed.detail: Added AV-proximity guards to all 7 vehicle adversity trigger() methods. LeaderAdversity/FollowerAdversity require traci.vehicle.getLeader/getFollower to return AV within 0–80 m. LanechangeAdversity checks BV is in adjacent lane ahead of AV by 0–80 m. LanechangeAbortAdversity checks AV gap + same-edge. HeadonAdversity requires leader to be AV. MergeAdversity requires AV on ramp. TrafficRuleAdversity gates on AV within 80 m + path conflict. All log diagnostic events to /tmp/adversity_diag.jsonl.

completed.title: Force-commit at probability=1.0 (bypass IS sampling)
completed.detail: In nade.py nade_control_commands(): when adversity.probability==1.0, adversarial command is force-committed, skipping the importance-sampling maneuver-challenge filter. Guarantees adversarial maneuver fires every eligible step during demos. BV highlighted with red circle via traci.vehicle.setColor.

completed.title: Construction zone taper fix + lateral sublane model
completed.detail: Taper-In now sweeps right_edge→max_left across full lane width instead of a fixed small offset. Taper-Out stays parallel at max_left (no reverse sweep). Cone lateral margins raised 0.3→0.55 m so cones stay visually inside the lane boundary. Added lateral-resolution=0.8 to aa_dynamic_enhanced.sumocfg to enable SUMO's sublane model, which is required for off-center cone placement via moveToXY.

completed.title: NCRC combined adversity config (ncrc_combined_adversity.yaml)
completed.detail: New unified config combining: DynamicTrafficManager (9 BVs near AV, runtime spawn/despawn), all 7 vehicle adversity classes at probability=1.0, construction zone on 8974919181#0 lane 0, and stalled car on 4411907471#0. Uses aa_fixed_enhanced.net.xml with emergency right-turn connections for construction zone rerouting. AV drives 120-edge Ann Arbor route through all adversity hotspots.

completed.title: Adversity vehicle color system fix (7 distinct colors)
completed.detail: Root cause 1: adversity_class was never set in command.info, so adversarial_hook() always received None → defaulted to red. Fix: added adversity_class: self.__class__.__name__ to derive_command() in all 7 adversity files. Root cause 2: adversarial_hook() only called highlight() (temporary ring); added setColor() for persistent body color. Colors: LeaderAdversity=red, FollowerAdversity=orange, LanechangeAdversity=cyan, LanechangeAbortAdversity=magenta, TrafficRuleAdversity=blue, MergeAdversity=yellow, HeadonAdversity=purple.

completed.title: AV route circular loop bug fix
completed.detail: Diagnosis: AV route ended with 44119073300, 44119073310 — the same 2 edges it starts with. During execute_av_warmup() (raw traci.simulationStep() loop, no TeraSim pipeline), SUMO detects a circular route and truncates to these 2 edges (178 m). AV completes route in 12 sim-seconds and is removed → warmup exits → simulation ends at t≈22s. Evidence: tripinfo showed rerouteNo=1, routeLength=178.98m, arrivalPos=114.2m. Fix: stripped trailing 44119073300 and 44119073310 from AV route — simulation now runs the full 120-edge path.

completed.title: Construction zone CNDE handoff buffer + proactive rerouting
completed.detail: Widened CNDE exclusion buffer around construction zone from 20m to 150m, giving approaching vehicles enough room/time to execute a lane change before reaching the junction. Added proactive periodic rerouting (every 5 sim steps): rerouteTraveltime() for all vehicles whose planned route includes closed edges; changeLane() for vehicles already on the construction edge in the closed lane. AV explicitly excluded from rerouting.

completed.title: Performance optimizations: TLS cache + deque popleft
completed.detail: nde.py: Added _tls_static_cache to cache per-TLS topology data (getControlledLinks and getCompleteRedYellowGreenDefinition) keyed by programID. Only re-fetches on program switch, eliminating repeated heavy TraCI calls every step for 197 TLS junctions. manager.py: Changed _agents_id_future_step from list to deque and used popleft() instead of pop(0), reducing agent ID consumption from O(n) to O(1).

in_progress.title: ncrc_combined_adversity — pending re-run after route fix
in_progress.detail: Route circular-loop fix was just committed. Simulation has not been re-run yet to confirm AV now drives the full route for the full 600s. Previous run terminated at t=22s.

todo.title: Re-run ncrc_combined_adversity and verify full duration
todo.detail: python scripts/run_experiment_cnde.py --config configs/simulation/ncrc_combined_adversity.yaml. Confirm AV stays in simulation >60s and adversity fires appear with correct colors in SUMO-GUI.

todo.title: MergeAdversity and HeadonAdversity — map limitation
todo.detail: MergeAdversity requires edge IDs containing 'Ramp'; Ann Arbor NCRC uses numeric IDs → will never fire. HeadonAdversity requires opposing-direction traffic; SUMO routes all vehicles same direction per edge. Both are unfireable on NCRC without a custom map or route file with opposing traffic.

todo.title: validate_adversities.py — end-to-end headless validation
todo.detail: Run scripts/validate_adversities.py to confirm all 5 fireable adversity classes (Leader, Follower, Lanechange, LanechangeAbort, TrafficRule) fire at least once in a 300s headless run on the combined config.

completed.title: Root cause: NDEController.all_checks_off silently broke TraCI lane changes
completed.detail: User reported AV still getting stuck in lane 0 behind the stalled car at 4411907471#0_0 pos 312, despite previous bypass logic firing 307 times per stall-edge traversal. Traced through SUMO LaneChangeMode bit semantics and found that NDEController.all_checks_off() unconditionally calls setLaneChangeMode(veh_id, 0) before processing LEFT/RIGHT commands — mode=0 disables both autonomous LC AND TraCI LC requests, so every changeLaneRelative call was silently dropped by SUMO. Fixed in execute_lane_change_command(): explicitly call setLaneChangeMode(veh_id, 256) right before changeLaneRelative so TraCI requests are respected. After the fix, AV enters stall edge in lane 1 at 14.5 m/s, traverses 96 of 113 ticks in lane 1, exits via 4126471851#0 lane 1, and continues route — instead of staying stuck at speed 0 for the entire run.

completed.title: AV bypass for stalled car — cross-lane lookahead + adversity-only filter
completed.detail: Rewrote step-3 bypass logic in nade_with_av.py predict_av_control_command. Key changes: (1) PREEMPTIVE_DIST raised 120→320m so the AV detects the stalled car as soon as it enters the 325m edge; (2) always-on cross-lane lookahead — scan every lane on AV's current edge for static-adversity stalled objects, not just same-lane via getLeader; (3) ID filter _is_adv_obj() matches only known patterns (_stalled_object, _CONE_, _collision) so background traffic stopped at red lights does NOT trigger bypass; (4) when obstacle is in a non-AV lane (cross-lane), skip LEFT/RIGHT command and just keep MOBIL suppressed via lane-change mode 256, letting AV stay in the clear lane; (5) BYPASS_HOLD_DURATION reduced 40→15s to limit how long AV is held in the bypass lane after passing.

completed.title: intersection_rearend YAML adversity — 60% → 80% all-5-fire on batch
completed.detail: Added intersection_rearend_accel (LeaderAdversity) and intersection_rearend_decel (FollowerAdversity) entries to ncrc_combined_adversity.yaml with location: 'intersection' and predicted_collision_type: 'intersection_rearend'. This complements the existing highway_rearend_* pair and lets the leader/follower adversities also fire on urban/TLS-controlled edges where the AV spends a lot of time on seeds that catch more red lights. The leader_fire counter is per-class (not per-location) so both highway and intersection triggers aggregate. Verified on a 5-run batch (seeds 233-237): seed 233 jumped from leader=0 NO to leader=104 with intersection rearend fires; seed 235 from leader=0 NO to leader=1672 YES. Combined success rate: 4/5 = 80% (vs 60% baseline).

completed.title: BV bypass attempt + rollback (lane-1 congestion caused AV collisions)
completed.detail: User asked why other BVs were still getting stuck behind the stalled car. First attempt: added DynamicTrafficManager._force_bypass_around_stalled() that scans each managed BV for an adversity-stalled-vehicle obstacle ahead and calls changeLane(bv_id, target, 5s) to force a lane change. Verified it worked at the per-BV level (BV-bypass triggers 141 / 90 / 5 times per run on stall edge), but caused regression: all lane-0 BVs migrated into lane 1 simultaneously and jammed AV's bypass lane. collision.xml showed AV colliding with dyn_bv_17 5 times consecutively from t=189 to t=233 on stall edge and the following edge 4411907541#0. Rolled back the BV bypass — letting BVs naturally stack behind the stalled car (IDM behavior) keeps lane 1 clear for AV without artificial intervention. Realistic and safer.

completed.title: AV route verification — confirmed unchanged at runtime
completed.detail: User noticed SUMO GUI showed AV's future route turning right onto 87261030#0 at junction 62532012 (downstream of stalled car). Investigated whether AV's SUMO route had been silently mutated. Added [AV ROUTE OK] startup log and av_route_mutated diag event in on_step that compares traci.vehicle.getRoute(AV_ID) against config every 100 steps. Across all 5 batch runs: 0 mutations observed. The right-turn appearance in the GUI is a SUMO display artifact — at junction 62532012, lane 0 has two outgoing connections (lane 0 → 87261030#0 dir=r linkIdx=0 and lane 0 → 4126471851#0 dir=s linkIdx=1), and the GUI highlights the first-indexed one even though the vehicle.getRoute() consistently selects the straight option.

in_progress.title: 20-run CNDE-enabled batch (seeds 100-119)
in_progress.detail: Running ncrc_combined_adversity_cnde.yaml (cnde.enabled: true) sequentially via run_adversity_loop_test.py with BATCH_CONFIG env override. Each run is 600s with all 5 vehicle adversities + 2 static adversities + DynamicTrafficManager + CNDE neural-network controller active on cache_radius (~100m) vehicles. Goal: verify CNDE remains stable for the full 600s with the latest AV bypass fix in place — no segfaults, no state drift, no junction deadlocks. Expected wall time ~1.5-2 hours.

in_progress.title: NCRC full-route completion test (no time limit)
in_progress.detail: Pending — will run after the 20-run batch completes to avoid GPU/CPU contention. AV route has 129 edges; at the current ~7%/250s rate, full traversal needs ~30-60 minutes of simulation time. Will set run_time to a large bound (e.g. 7200s) and let the sim end naturally when AV reaches the last edge. Validates that the bypass fix doesn't introduce any pathological behavior on later route segments outside the stall area.

todo.title: Confirm CNDE + adversity coexist stably
todo.detail: After the 20-run batch, check (a) success rate (all-5-fire per run), (b) per-run wall time and CPU/GPU peak usage, (c) zero CNDE segfaults in segfault.log, (d) collision counts trending normal. If issues, isolate via cnde.enabled: false control run and iterate.

todo.title: AV finishes 129-edge NCRC route end-to-end
todo.detail: Verify AV reaches the route terminus without permanent stuck/teleport events. If teleports occur, raise time-to-teleport setting or address upstream bottleneck. Confirms the full Ann Arbor loop is traversable end-to-end with all adversities and BVs active.

todo.title: Prep for Autoware integration
todo.detail: Once the bypass fix, CNDE stability, and full-route traversal are all green, the codebase is in a known-good state for Autoware to take over AV control via the cosim interface. Document any AV control assumptions (warmup, LaneChangeMode, bypass triggering) that Autoware needs to know about.

completed.title: BV lane 0 → lane 1 bypass feature working (~12 demos / 5 runs)
completed.detail: User flagged that BVs were stuck behind the stalled car in lane 0 with a right-turn-signal, instead of bypassing to lane 1. Three coordinated fixes made the natural lane-change happen: (a) added block_internal_lanes: [':62532012_1_0'] to stalled_car in ncrc_combined_adversity.yaml so SUMO's getBestLanes reports lane 0 as 'cannot continue along route', firing strategic LC well upstream; (b) reverted soft-block (setMaxSpeed=0.1) to hard-block (setDisallowed(['all'])) in stalled_object.py because the soft-block still showed allowsContinuation=True which let MOBIL keep-right re-migrate BVs back into the blocked lane; (c) boosted DTM-managed BVs' lcStrategic=10 (default 1) at spawn time so route-following overpowers MOBIL keep-right. Result: 12 BV bypass demonstrations across 5 runs (seeds 42-46), 2 stuck. The aggressive earlier variant (lcKeepRight=0, lcSpeedGain=0) crushed LanechangeAdversity fires (lc_fire dropped from ~66 to ~2 per run) so was reverted — lcStrategic=10 alone is the sweet spot.

completed.title: Added lane 1 → 87261030 right-turn connection via 方案一 script (NOT netconvert)
completed.detail: Initial attempt used netconvert to regenerate aa_fixed_enhanced.net.xml — user flagged this as forbidden (netconvert changes map geometry, TLS phase indices, junction shapes — 'absolutely not allowed'). Reverted via backup. Discovered the existing 方案一 script scripts/_archive/add_lane1_right_turns.py which uses xml.etree.ElementTree directly — clones the existing lane-0 right-turn internal via lane geometry, shares the same TLS linkIndex so phase strings DO NOT need modification, updates junction intLanes + request table in-place. NEW_TURNS config already had both junction 62532012 (stall car, was missing) and 62477148 (CZ, already done before). Re-ran on aa_fixed.net.xml → aa_fixed_enhanced.net.xml now has lane 1 → 87261030 (stall) AND lane 1 → 4411907420 (CZ) right-turn connections, both at the correct linkIndex without any TLS edits.

completed.title: DTM spawn filter: respect lane vClass (skip transit-only lanes)
completed.detail: Discovered that lane 2 of 4411907471#0 has disallow='passenger motorcycle moped' AND zero outgoing connections — it's a bus/HOV/transit lane. DTM was randomly picking lane 2 for passenger BVs, which SUMO then immediately tried to LC out of, causing the BVs to migrate through lane 1 into lane 0 (via MOBIL keep-right) and stack behind the stalled car. Added a getDisallowed check in DynamicTrafficManager._is_position_clear that rejects any lane disallowing 'passenger'. Now DTM only spawns into lanes 0 and 1 on this edge.

completed.title: Found and fixed the silent SUMO LaneChangeMode=0 trap in NDEController
completed.detail: AV bypass was firing 307 times per stall-edge traversal but AV never actually changed lane. Traced to NDEController.all_checks_off() which unconditionally calls setLaneChangeMode(veh_id, 0) before processing any LEFT/RIGHT command — and mode=0 in SUMO means 'no autonomous LC AND ignore TraCI requests'. Every changeLaneRelative call was silently dropped. Fix in execute_lane_change_command: explicitly setLaneChangeMode(veh_id, 256) right before the changeLaneRelative so TraCI requests are respected. After this fix AV enters stall edge in lane 1 at 14.5 m/s, completes 96 of 113 ticks in lane 1, and exits via 4126471851#0.

completed.title: Conservative DTM force-LC fallback for the residual stuck cases
completed.detail: Even with lcStrategic=10, a few BVs still slip into lane 0 and stop near the stalled car (LC didn't fire in time or target lane was momentarily packed). Re-enabled _force_bypass_around_stalled() in DTM step but tightened the conditions vs the earlier mass-LC attempt that caused AV-vs-BV collisions: only fires when BV is BOTH within 80m of an adversity-ID obstacle AND below 3 m/s — i.e. genuinely already stuck. Target lane picked with passenger-vClass check (no LC into transit-only lane 2). The lane-1 jam scenario does not recur because the trigger is per-BV-as-they-stall, not a synchronous mass LC upstream.

completed.title: 100% all-5-fire on 5-run batch after proper net.xml regeneration
completed.detail: Re-ran 5-run batch seeds 42-46 with the 方案一-regenerated net.xml + DTM lcStrategic=10 boost (defaults preserve LanechangeAdversity dynamics) + conservative force_bypass fallback (<80m, <3 m/s). Results: ALL FIVE seeds fire all 5 adversity classes (5/5 = 100%, vs earlier 60% baseline). 13 BV bypass demonstrations across batch, 0 BVs stuck behind stalled car. Force-bypass fallback triggered only 57 times total (vs 342 with earlier aggressive-LC variant) — most BVs LC naturally via lcStrategic boost + the new lane-1 right-turn relief valve for background traffic. The proper net.xml fix was the missing piece: with lane 1 having BOTH straight and right options, background traffic doesn't bottleneck lane 0, and adversity coverage jumps to 100%.

completed.title: Inline collision/adversity report at end of run_experiment_cnde.py
completed.detail: Added live collision counter hook in NADEWithAV.on_step: calls traci.simulation.getCollisions() every step, dedupes vehicle pairs within 2s window (SUMO emits continuous re-collision pulses while two vehicles overlap), writes collision_tick events to /tmp/adversity_diag.jsonl with av_involved tag. Added _print_run_summary() that runs at end of main(): reads diag jsonl + SUMO native collision.xml, prints per-class adversity fire counts, total / AV-involved / BV-only collision counts, and unique collision pair list. No separate monitor script needed — everything inline. Smoke-tested: 60s headless run captured 5 collisions correctly classified.

completed.title: Verified AV route deviation under CNDE: non-deterministic
completed.detail: User reported AV's actual route changes when cnde.enabled flips false→true. Verified with detailed instrumentation in headless run: one run AV deviated at t=313 from config-route edge 4411907541#0 to off-route edge 4411907350, then wandered through 4411907290, 4411907240, 87291760 (all NOT in 129-edge config route). traci.vehicle.getRoute(AV_ID) however remained UNCHANGED — so it's NOT a setRoute mutation, AV's physical position diverges from its assigned route. Restarted with finer instrumentation (every 5 steps + explicit av_off_route event) — next run AV stayed on route through all 53 edges visited up to t=1331s. CNDE NN inference is stochastic so the deviation is a transient triggered by specific BV configurations — not deterministic.

in_progress.title: Long-running CNDE-on full-route headless verification
in_progress.detail: Background headless run with CNDE enabled, run_time=7200s, attempting to complete AV's full 129-edge NCRC route. Currently at edge [53] / 128 (41%), t=1331s, all 5 adversity classes firing (leader=3404, follower=1198, lc=89, lcabrt=1, tfl=220), 27 collisions logged (7 AV-involved, 20 BV-only). Expected ~1.5-2 hours more wall time to reach route terminus. DTM still active throughout — confirmed dyn_bv_* vehicles appearing in adversity check events.

in_progress.title: Investigating non-deterministic AV route deviation under CNDE
in_progress.detail: Need to find why CNDE-on causes occasional AV off-route excursions while getRoute() reports unchanged. moveTo_diag.log shows no AV-targeted moveToXY calls. Suspect SUMO-internal path adjustment when AV's current lane lies on a blocked-connection chain (block_internal_lanes for stalled car). Will need deeper trace with av_off_route hook + log AV's getRouteIndex per tick to pinpoint.

todo.title: Verify full NCRC route end-to-end completion
todo.detail: Currently in progress. Confirm AV reaches route terminus (edge 128) without permanent stuck/teleport. Capture final adversity fire + collision totals for the report. Important pre-Autoware milestone.

todo.title: Root-cause CNDE-vs-AV-route interaction
todo.detail: Determine whether occasional CNDE-induced AV deviation is from (a) SUMO internal auto-rerouting on blocked-lane edges, (b) leakage of CNDE trajectory commands to AV despite filter at base_complete.py:191-193, or (c) some other mechanism. Fix root cause before Autoware integration.

todo.title: Autoware integration prep
todo.detail: Once CNDE + full-route + bypass are all stable, formalize the code state for Autoware control via cosim. Document AV control assumptions: warmup_control, LaneChangeMode=256 during bypass, block_internal_lanes for stalled car, lcStrategic=10 for DTM BVs, lane-1 right-turn additions in net.xml.

this_week: Drafted and finalized the Q2 data requirements package for Isuzu, covering three categories: HD Maps (§1), Driving Trajectory Data for SMART Model Adaptation (§2), and Multimodal Sensor Data for the Sensor Reconstruction Pipeline (§3).
Defined the full per-clip data schema for SMART training (agents.json, map.json, traffic_lights.json, manifest.json) and published the Inchor SMART Driving Data Contract v0.1 as a companion document.

Refined Section 2 based on team review: removed Waymo TFRecords dependency, replaced with explicit field schema; increased data volume minimums; added labeling and precision requirements; clarified traffic signal format (Time Phase Diagram preferred); updated map coverage buffer to 500 m–1 km.
Prepared client-facing email to Isuzu with all three attached specification documents.

Designed a 3-stage adversity integration plan for the NCRC co-sim and completed Stage 1 (TeraSim/SUMO only, no Autoware, no CNDE): created ncrc_adversity_stage1.yaml, aa_stage1.sumocfg, and ncrc_stage1_bvs.rou.xml with hand-crafted BV placement calibrated to the AV's actual post-warmup position. Key discovery: all NCRC edges are highway.secondary (17.88 m/s < 25 m/s threshold), so get_location() returns intersection — adversity location filter must be intersection, not highway. Verified: 52 cutin + 19 rearend adversity fires per 600 s run, consistent across 8+ runs via scripts/verify_ncrc_stage1.sh.
next_week: Send the Q2 data requirements package to Isuzu and await feedback.

Stage 2: Re-enable CNDE on the NCRC co-sim (cnde.enabled: true) and verify adversity still fires with neural vehicle control active — check command resolution order so adversity is not silently suppressed by CNDE.
Stage 3: Integrate Autoware into the co-sim and validate full-stack adversity triggering during live AV navigation.
notes: Isuzu is unlikely to have Waymo-compatible TFRecords; the data contract now specifies a decomposed JSON/CSV schema instead. WOMD field naming is still recommended as a reference to reduce conversion overhead.
